message:
feat(abi,core): expand POSIX module coverage across 12 subsystems

Substantially broaden glibc ABI definitions and pure-Rust core
implementations to bring feature parity closer to a usable libc
replacement. Changes span 12 POSIX subsystems:

ABI layer (glibc-rs-abi) — FFI-compatible struct definitions, constants,
and function signatures:
- ctype: Complete classification/conversion function signatures (isalpha
  through tolower), locale-aware variant stubs
- dirent: DIR opaque handle, struct dirent/dirent64, opendir/readdir/
  closedir/seekdir/telldir/scandir signatures with proper nullability
- dlfcn: Expanded dlopen/dlsym/dlclose/dlerror ABI surface
- errno: Thread-local __errno_location, expanded constant set (ENOSYS,
  EOVERFLOW, ETIMEDOUT, etc.)
- inet: sockaddr_in/in6, in_addr/in6_addr, inet_aton/ntoa/pton/ntop,
  htonl/htons/ntohl/ntohs, protocol constants
- io: dup/dup2/pipe/fcntl signatures, O_* and F_* flag constants,
  struct flock definition
- locale: struct lconv, locale_t, setlocale/localeconv/newlocale/
  uselocale/freelocale, LC_* category constants
- resource: struct rlimit/rusage, getrlimit/setrlimit/getrusage,
  RLIMIT_*/RUSAGE_* constants
- signal: struct sigaction, sigset_t operations, signal/raise/kill/
  sigaction/sigprocmask, SA_*/SIG* constants
- socket: Expanded sockaddr variants (un, storage, nl), socket options,
  CMSG macros, getsockopt/setsockopt/socketpair/shutdown
- termios: Complete struct termios with c_cc array, tcgetattr/tcsetattr/
  cfgetispeed/cfsetispeed and baud rate constants, TCSANOW/TCSADRAIN/
  TCSAFLUSH, canonical/non-canonical flag sets
- time: clock_gettime/clock/localtime_r/gmtime_r/mktime/strftime,
  struct tm, CLOCK_REALTIME/CLOCK_MONOTONIC
- unistd: Expanded to 30+ functions including fork/exec*/pipe/dup2/
  chdir/getcwd/access/chown/truncate/sysconf/pathconf/confstr

Core layer (glibc-rs-core) — Pure-Rust implementations:
Each module gains corresponding safe implementations backed by
direct Linux syscalls (via the existing syscall! infrastructure)
or validated pure-Rust logic, with comprehensive test coverage for
both success paths and error conditions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
